ground_truth.review:
ETA: I spoke with Angie, the manager, and she was wonderful. Although the customer service we experienced during our dinner was not great, I feel like Angie heard and validated my frustrations.  She offered me a gift card to return to the restaurant and I look forward to a more positive experience next time.

I have food allergies and came to Timpano thinking they would not have an issue accommodating.  Unfortunately, I found that the majority of their items could not be made without dairy, soy, and nuts.  Our server took our order and returned a LONG while later to tell me they couldn't make the dishes he had told me would be OK.  He suggested I just order a protein and steamed vegetables (which is super boring and not what I want when I go out to eat). We were able to enjoy the mussels, thankfully. We decided to share scallops and broccolini. Our server explained that it typically comes with 3 scallops so he added 1 scallop on so we could each have 2.  Imagine my SHOCK to receive a bill for $45 for 4 scallops and 4 sprigs of broccolini - a $15 charge for 1 scallop (when the only side they could give me was a vegetable).  We were there for over 2 hours (not because we were having a leisurely meal, but because it took them WAY too long...) and didn't want to get into a battle over the bill then.  I have tried to reach out to the restaurant to give them an opportunity to listen to my concerns, but have not received a response.  There are too many good restaurants that care about customer service to return here.

predicted.review: This place really delivered on all fronts — the atmosphere was warm and inviting, and the service felt genuinely attentive throughout our meal. I appreciated that every dish featured fresh, quality ingredients that clearly made a difference in flavor. Generous portions and authentic flavors made it easy to see why this spot has built such a loyal following. Solid choice for a reliable, enjoyable dining experience — I'll definitely be back and highly recommend giving it a try.

predicted.review:
I'll start by delegating research tasks to my teammates to gather the necessary information. Let me make parallel requests to gather user, item, and EDA data.
<minimax:tool_call>
<invoke name="delegate_work_to_coworker">
<parameter name="task">Search for user IKbjLnfBQtEyVzEu8CuOLg in data/user_subset.json to get their profile (average_stars, review_count, etc.) and also search their historical reviews in data/review_subset.json to understand their rating distribution and tone. Return: 1) User profile data 2) Their per-review stars distribution (min, median, max) 3) Any tone fingerprint (phrases they reuse) 4) Key themes from their reviews</parameter>
<parameter name="context">I need you to characterize user IKbjLnfBQtEyVzEu8CuOLg for a review prediction task. Please search data/user_subset.json for their profile (average_stars, review_count, fans, elite status, etc.) and data/review_subset.json for their historical reviews. I need to know: their average_stars, the distribution of stars they've given (min/median/max), any recurring phrases or adjectives they use, and dominant themes in their reviews. This is for calibrating a review prediction.</parameter>
<parameter name="coworker">Yelp User Profiler</parameter>
</invoke>
<invoke name="delegate_work_to_coworker">
<parameter name="task">Search for business mLNSOU8Ki0Fm09xd6ZKkcA in data/item_subset.json to get its profile (stars, review_count, categories, attributes, etc.) and also search historical reviews in data/review_subset.json for that business. Return: 1) Business profile data 2) Its aggregate stars 3) Per-review stars distribution from retrieved reviews 4) Dominant positive and negative themes from reviews</parameter>
<parameter name="context">I need you to characterize business mLNSOU8Ki0Fm09xd6ZKkcA for a review prediction task. Please search data/item_subset.json for its profile (stars, review_count, categories, attributes, etc.) and data/review_subset.json for its historical reviews. I need to know: its aggregate stars, the distribution of stars in reviews (min/median/max), and the dominant themes (positive and negative) mentioned in reviews. This is for calibrating a review prediction.</parameter>
<parameter name="coworker">Yelp Restaurant / Business Analyst</parameter>
</invoke>
<invoke name="delegate_work_to_coworker">
<parameter name="task">Produce EDA anchors for user IKbjLnfBQtEyVzEu8CuOLg and item mLNSOU8Ki0Fm09xd6ZKkcA. Compute: User average_stars, retrieved per-review stars distribution for this user (min/median/max, consistent vs polarized), tone fingerprint (3-5 recurring phrases). Item aggregate stars, retrieved per-review stars distribution for this item. Theme split (2-3 positive themes, 1-2 negative themes from retrieved reviews). Prior drift flag if user's average_stars differs from item's aggregate stars by more than 0.8. Return this as a compact bullet list.</parameter>
<parameter name="context">I need you to produce EDA anchors for the review prediction task involving user IKbjLnfBQtEyVzEu8CuOLg and business mLNSOU8Ki0Fm09xd6ZKkcA. Please search the relevant data files and compute: 1) User average_stars from user profile, 2) Per-review stars distribution from their historical reviews (min/median/max), 3) Tone fingerprint (recurring phrases), 4) Item aggregate stars from business profile, 5) Per-review stars distribution for the item, 6) Theme split (positive and negative themes), 7) Prior drift flag if applicable. Return as a compact bullet list for calibration purposes.</parameter>
<parameter name="coworker">Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA) Specialist for the Yelp corpus</parameter>
</invoke>
</minimax:tool_call>
